prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very close, cropped view of a large white, stylized wordmark on a dark background. Only part of the lettering is visible, appearing to read “ZEN…” and suggesting the Zenith logo, though the full word is not shown in this sampled frame.

By the final frame, the commercial has shifted to a product display. Against a dark studio-like background, two wood-cabinet television sets are shown side by side. Each TV screen displays a small image of a person in a light/yellow top, though the activity is not clear from the frame. Above the televisions is prominent on-screen branding text: “ZENITH,” “SOLID STATE,” and “CHROMACOLOR II.” Near the bottom, small white text reads: “The quality goes in before the name goes on.”

No other actions or transitions are visible in the sampled frames.
